title: Kinder Scout (2) Avro Anson Memorial
location_name: Kinder Scout (2), Peak District (Derbyshire / South Pennines)
crew_summary: All survived; Flt Lt Chelstowski and crew received minor injuries
memorial_type: Crash site marker; engine and panels remain
narrative: On 11 December 1944, Avro Anson N9853 of No. 16 Flying Training School took off from RAF Newton near Nottingham on a training flight to RAF Millom in Cumbria. The aircraft was crewed by members of the Polish Air Force under RAF command. Flight Lieutenant Aleksander Chelstowski, an experienced Polish pilot, immediately became lost in low cloud after departure. Knowing the Peak District lay between Nottingham and his destination, Chelstowski descended below the cloud to obtain a visual fix, but had not expected to have travelled so far west. The aircraft struck the snow-covered moor on Edale Moor, Kinder Scout. Remarkably, all crew survived the crash with only minor injuries. They were led off the hill and transferred to the RAF Hospital at Wilmslow for treatment before being returned to Newton. The subsequent inquiry found Chelstowski guilty of gross negligence, and his commanding officer recommended he never fly again. Despite this, Chelstowski went on to have a very distinguished remainder of the war and was decorated four times. In 1955, after a request from the newly formed Peak District National Park, an RAF recovery team visited the crash site and others on Kinder to clean up wreckage, though engine and panel remains are still visible today.

crew.name: Aleksander Chelstowski
crew.fate_detail: Survived with minor injuries. Found guilty of gross negligence by the subsequent inquiry, but went on to be decorated four times during the remainder of the war.
crew.bio: An experienced Polish pilot serving with the RAF. Despite being found guilty of gross negligence for this crash and his CO recommending he never fly again, Chelstowski went on to have a distinguished career and was decorated four times.

crew.name: Second crew member (name not recorded in available sources)
crew.fate_detail: Survived with minor injuries. Treated at RAF Hospital Wilmslow.

cause_of_loss: Pilot descended below cloud in poor visibility and struck snow-covered moor; navigational error
memorial_details: Engine and panel remains are still visible at the crash site on Edale Moor, Kinder Scout. The site is accessible to experienced fell walkers via routes from Edale. An RAF recovery team cleaned up some wreckage in 1955 at the request of the Peak District National Park Authority.

external_narratives.summary: Research indicates this relates to an Avro Anson crash on Kinder Scout involving Polish crew from a flying exercise on 11 December 1944. Flt Lt Aleksander Chelstowski took off from RAF Newton near Nottingham but became lost in low cloud. He descended below the cloud but struck the snow-covered moor. All crew survived the crash.
key_details: Aircraft was an Avro Anson Mk.I, serial N9853
Chelstowski found guilty of gross negligence but later decorated four times

external_narratives.summary: Crash database entry for Avro Anson N9853 on Edale Moor, Kinder Scout. Unit: No. 16 FTS, RAF Newton. Four of those onboard received minor injuries. After treatment at RAF Hospital Wilmslow they were returned to Newton.
key_details: Flying from RAF Newton to Millom

external_narratives.summary: Comprehensive listing of Peak District aircraft crash sites including the Kinder Scout Polish crew incident. The blog documents engine and panel remains still visible at the site and provides grid references for walkers.
key_details: Engine and panels remain at crash site
